Title: Pandemonium Breaks Out: Disruption of Salicylic Acid-Mediated Defense by Plant Pathogens

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissues; chloroplast-derived SA accumulates locally and systemically during defense
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for SA accumulation and systemic acquired resistance by exporting SA synthesized in the chloroplast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS5/SID1 encodes a MATE-family transporter required for SA accumulation. 2. It exports SA synthesized in the chloroplast through the ICS1 pathway. 3. eds5 mutants show compromised pathogen resistance, reduced systemic PR gene expression, and failure to develop SAR.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; pathogen infection phenotyping; gene expression analysis
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering stronger or pathogen-insensitive EDS5 function may enhance SA accumulation and SAR, but must consider growth-immunity balance.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA pathway activity can impose fitness costs.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DEL1 suppresses EDS5 expression; JA can induce EDS5 while repressing PAD4, creating network-dependent effects on SA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection; jasmonic acid network perturbation

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator of pathogen-induced SA metabolism; disruption decreases SA-glucoside and disease resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PBS3/WIN3/GDG1 is essential for pathogen-induced SA metabolism. 2. pbs3 mutants are more susceptible to virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae strains. 3. PBS3 expression correlates with ICS1 expression and disruption greatly decreases SA-glucoside accumulation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; pathogen infection assays; in vitro enzyme assay
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant immune signaling and storage as SA-glucoside
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to reinforce pathogen-induced SA metabolism upstream of defense activation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SA is a poor substrate and inhibitor of PBS3 in vitro; together with EPS1 proposed to fulfill an IPL-equivalent role in the Arabidopsis ICS pathway.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Pathogen-induced; highly correlated with ICS1 expression

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for full pathogen-induced total SA accumulation, PR gene expression, and resistance
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EPS1 mutants show compromised resistance to virulent and avirulent Pseudomonas syringae strains. 2. eps1 mutants have reduced PR gene expression and reduced total SA accumulation. 3. EPS1 is hypothesized to function with PBS3 in the ICS pathway as an IPL-equivalent component.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; pathogen assays; PR gene expression and SA measurement
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target to reinforce SA biosynthesis branch efficiency.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Precise enzymatic contribution to SA biosynthesis and defense remained unresolved in this review.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Dose-dependent activator of SA accumulation and SA-dependent cell death/defense
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gain-of-function acd6-1 shows elevated SA, spontaneous cell death, constitutive defense, and increased resistance to P. syringae. 2. ACD6 is necessary for defense activation in a dose-dependent manner. 3. ACD6 links SA biosynthesis/signaling with cell-death-associated immunity.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense signaling; supports resistance and cell-death-associated immunity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could strengthen disease resistance, but manipulation must avoid excessive cell death and growth penalties.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Spontaneous cell death and likely growth defects/fitness costs when overactivated.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents a cell-death-associated positive regulator upstream of SA pathway amplification.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator required for normal SA accumulation during pathogen infection and ETI output
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NDR1 is important for ETI involving CC-NB-LRR R proteins. 2. NDR1 associates with RIN4. 3. Disruption of NDR1 reduces SA accumulation after pathogen infection.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; interaction studies; pathogen assays
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for strengthening ETI-linked SA accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions in resistance signaling rather than direct catalysis.

Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Canonical SA-responsive defense output gene with antimicrobial function downstream of SA signaling
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PR1 is strongly induced by SA and pathogen infection. 2. PR1 is widely used as a marker of SA signaling. 3. PR1 has sterol-binding antimicrobial activity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene expression assays; protein functional characterization
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful downstream marker and possible defense effector deployment target.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is a recurring core SA-defense output in the review, though not a biosynthetic regulator.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced upon biotrophic and semi-biotrophic pathogen infection

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to isochorismate in the chloroplast for pathogen-induced SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Chloroplast-derived SA in infected leaves and systemic tissues
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Major biosynthetic enzyme required for pathogen-induced SA accumulation and defense
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ICS1 pathway is the main route for pathogen-induced SA biosynthesis in Arabidopsis. 2. ICS1 converts chorismate to isochorismate in chloroplasts. 3. Pathogen and host regulators converge on ICS1 expression, making it a major target of pathogen suppression.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; pathogen-induced SA profiling; expression studies
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prime target to enhance SA-based immunity, but must balance resistance and fitness costs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess SA pathway activation may reduce growth/fitness.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Directly repressed indirectly by coronatine-triggered NAC TFs; targeted upstream by pathogen isochorismatases and HopI1-mediated chloroplast disruption.
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: SARD1; CBP60g; NTL9; CHE; TCP8; TCP9; ANAC019; ANAC055; ANAC072
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Pathogen-induced, including guard cells and systemic tissues depending on regulator
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Pathogen infection; SA pathway activation; coronatine/JA signaling

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates/degrades salicylic acid to catechol, which cannot activate plant defense
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with and destroys target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduces host SA accumulation to support pathogen growth
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. U. maydis contains three putative SA hydroxylase genes, with Um05230 validated as active. 2. Um05230 is induced during pathogenic development. 3. It is required for growth on SA-containing media, supporting a role in detoxifying host SA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Expression analysis; enzyme activity assay; growth on SA-containing plates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Supports development of inhibitors or non-degradable SA analogs for disease control.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pathogen enzyme, not plant gene; application value lies in inhibitor design.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Degrades salicylic acid to catechol, eliminating SA defense signaling capacity
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with and destroys target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Ectopic expression dramatically lowers SA levels and increases susceptibility to biotrophic and semi-biotrophic pathogens
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NahG encodes a bacterial SA-degrading enzyme. 2. Expression in Arabidopsis and tobacco markedly reduces SA content. 3. NahG plants are highly susceptible to biotrophic and semi-biotrophic pathogens.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic ectopic expression; SA quantification; disease assays
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Negative tool gene used experimentally to deplete SA; also motivates design of hydroxylase-resistant SA analogs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Strong loss of disease resistance due to SA depletion.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a classic experimental tool rather than beneficial engineering target.

Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: Nag pathway enzymes for salicylic acid degradation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Gentisic acid; maleylpyruvate; fumarylpyruvate; pyruvate; fumarate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Degrades SA to gentisic acid and subsequently to central metabolic intermediates, detoxifying SA and supporting pathogen fitness
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Competes with and destroys target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Protects the pathogen from SA toxicity and enhances virulence by catabolizing host SA
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. R. solanacearum degrades SA through the Nag pathway. 2. SA activates NagGH and NagAaAb genes. 3. Catabolism lowers SA toxicity and contributes to pathogen fitness and virulence in host plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathway characterization; virulence analysis; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate target for inhibitors that block pathogen SA catabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pathogen genes, not host nutrient-improvement genes; relevant because they directly alter SA metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Competing side-route from immediate SA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes isochorismate, diverting it away from SA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted effector delivered into host cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Decreases SA accumulation and promotes pathogen virulence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PsIsc1 is a secreted oomycete effector similar to known isochorismatases. 2. Overexpression in plants reduces SA level. 3. Purified protein hydrolyzes isochorismate in vitro and contributes to virulence.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene overexpression in plants; pathogen gene downregulation; in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests breeding or editing to avoid effector susceptibility and development of inhibitors against pathogen ISC activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pathogen effector enzyme acting inside host cells.

Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Competing side-route from immediate SA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyzes isochorismate, reducing precursor availability for SA production
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted effector delivered into host cells
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Lowers host SA accumulation and contributes to fungal virulence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. VdIsc1 is a Verticillium isochorismatase effector. 2. Altering its expression changes pathogen virulence. 3. Overexpression in plants lowers SA and purified protein hydrolyzes isochorismate in vitro.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene overexpression in plants; pathogen gene downregulation; enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for anti-effector strategies protecting SA biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Pathogen effector enzyme with strong mechanistic evidence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts chorismate to prephenate, competing with host SA biosynthesis for chorismate substrate
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Diverts precursor pool away from target product
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Secreted effector acting in host cytosol
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Limits host SA biosynthesis and accumulation, enhancing pathogen virulence
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. U. maydis secretes Cmu1 during maize infection. 2. Cmu1 competes with the host for chorismate in the cytosol. 3. Conversion of chorismate to prephenate limits SA biosynthesis and signaling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Pathogen infection studies; biochemical characterization referenced in review
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Suggests strategies to protect chorismate flux to SA during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts complementarily with U. maydis SA hydroxylase to suppress host SA and gain carbon resources.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: NPR family; ankyrin repeat-containing co-activator lacking DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf tissues during local and systemic defense
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Master positive regulator of SA signaling that activates PR gene expression and systemic acquired resistance
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Master regulator of SA-mediated local and systemic defense output
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: SA/redox-dependent nuclear translocation and transcriptional co-activation with TGA TFs
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: PR1; PR2; PR5; callose synthase genes; SA-regulated defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates PR and other defense-related genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salicylic acid; pathogen infection; cellular redox changes
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in SA signaling amplification; targeted by NPR3/NPR4 and AvrPtoB
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interacts with TGA TFs; targeted for degradation by AvrPtoB; associated with cullin3-adaptor circuitry via NPR3/NPR4
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR1 is the master regulator of SA-mediated plant defense. 2. SA-induced redox changes convert NPR1 oligomers to monomers that move to the nucleus. 3. NPR1 cooperates with TGA transcription factors to activate PR gene expression and is targeted by the bacterial effector AvrPtoB for degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genetic screens; localization studies; interaction assays; pathogen assays
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense against biotrophic, semi-biotrophic, and some viral pathogens; SAR
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for engineering durable immunity, including editing effector interaction or PTM sites to prevent pathogen-mediated degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excessive activation of SA defense can reduce growth or fitness.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review highlights NPR1 as central and a likely major pathogen target.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: TGA1; TGA2; TGA3; TGA4; TGA5; TGA6; TGA7; NPR3; NPR4; AvrPtoB; TCP8; TCP14; TCP15

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator facilitating ICS1 expression and SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Important positive regulator of pathogen-induced SA synthesis and SAR
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of positive defense amplification network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SARD1 facilitates ICS1 expression during defense. 2. It positively regulates SA biosynthesis. 3. TGA1/TGA4 and CHE contribute to its induction in defense and SAR contexts.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising positive regulator to boost SA production if tuned carefully.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Presented as one of several TFs controlling ICS1.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator facilitating ICS1 expression and SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major positive controller of SA synthesis upstream of ICS1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of positive defense amplification network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CBP60g positively regulates SA biosynthesis by facilitating ICS1 expression. 2. It acts with SARD1 in defense. 3. TGA1/TGA4 and CHE are required for its full induction in specific contexts.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential lever for boosting immune SA accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely defense-associated fitness costs if misregulated.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Highlighted with SARD1 as a major upstream SA biosynthesis regulator.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of ICS1, PAD4, and EDS1 induction in guard cells, promoting SA biosynthesis and stomatal defense
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential guard-cell regulator linking SA biosynthesis to stomatal closure
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates their expression in guard cells
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SA biosynthesis and stomatal immune defense
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Defense signaling; targeted by HopD1 in discussion
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NTL9 is essential for induction of ICS1, PAD4, and EDS1 in guard cells. 2. It positively regulates stomatal closure to prevent pathogen entry. 3. It is a likely pathogen effector target in SA defense suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for improving entry-point defense against foliar pathogens.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: HopD1 targeting of NTL9 is discussed as a future-relevant effector strategy.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA signaling and biosynthesis by cooperating with NPR1 and supporting SARD1/CBP60g induction
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator needed for full induction of upstream SA-biosynthetic regulators
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes induction of SARD1/CBP60g and PR1-related defense expression
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA1 is required for full induction of SARD1 and CBP60g in defense. 2. It interacts with NPR1 in SA-treated leaves. 3. TGA1 contributes to SA-responsive defense gene activation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Interaction assays; genetic analyses; expression studies
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial regulator for strengthening SA pathway outputs.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes induction of SARD1 and CBP60g to support SA biosynthesis and defense
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive regulator of upstream SA biosynthetic transcriptional network
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TGA4 is required for full induction of SARD1 and CBP60g. 2. It supports SA and pipecolic acid biosynthesis-related defense responses. 3. It works with TGA1 in the SA network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential positive regulator for improving immune robustness.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Context provided through cited work summarized in review.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates SARD1 and CBP60g expression in systemic tissues during SAR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Systemic regulator supporting SAR-associated SA biosynthetic competence
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CHE positively regulates SARD1 and CBP60g in systemic tissues. 2. It contributes to systemic acquired resistance. 3. It thereby supports the SA biosynthesis network.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target to improve systemic immunity.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitate ICS1 expression to positively regulate SA biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Positive transcriptional inputs into ICS1 control
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TCP8 and TCP9 facilitate ICS1 expression. 2. They positively regulate SA biosynthesis during defense. 3. TCP family members are important coordinated regulators of ICS1.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential nodes for coordinated enhancement of SA pathway.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The review also notes TCP14 and TCP15 as NPR1 interactors in future perspectives, but TCP8/TCP9 are the directly summarized SA biosynthesis regulators.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipase-like α/β hydrolase fold protein with non-catalytic immune function
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential positive regulator upstream of SA synthesis and ETI; stimulates SA production through ICS1 upregulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Indispensable immune node linking R-protein signaling to SA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1; PAD4-associated outputs; MYC2 suppression with PAD4
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Stimulates ICS1; promotes SA-dependent and SA-independent defense gene activation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback with SA because SA also induces EDS1 expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms complexes with PAD4 and SAG101
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EDS1 is an essential component of innate immunity and ETI upstream of SA synthesis and PCD. 2. EDS1 and PAD4 stimulate SA production through ICS1 upregulation and are themselves induced by SA, forming a positive feedback loop. 3. EDS1 forms indispensable complexes with PAD4 and SAG101 and can act through non-catalytic scaffold functions.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; protein interaction studies; overexpression studies
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important target for strengthening ETI/SAR, but network complexity and fitness effects require careful tuning.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible constitutive defense costs when overexpressed.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic residues are dispensable for immune function, supporting scaffold role.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: PAD4; SAG101; MYC2; AvrRps4; HopA1; RPS4; SRFR1

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Lipase-like α/β hydrolase fold protein with non-catalytic immune function
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator that stimulates SA production through ICS1 upregulation and contributes to SAR
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core signaling partner required for robust SA amplification
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: ICS1; EDS5-associated network; MYC2 suppression with EDS1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes ICS1 expression; supports EDS5 expression through network effects
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: ETI signaling; SA feedback; JA network antagonism
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedback with SA because SA induces PAD4 expression
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms complex with EDS1 and SAG101-related node
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PAD4 works with EDS1 to stimulate SA production through ICS1 upregulation. 2. PAD4 is itself induced by SA, creating a positive feedback loop. 3. PAD4 is crucial for SAR and is integrated with JA-EDS5 crosstalk.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; interaction studies; overexpression referenced
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for improving SA-mediated disease resistance if tradeoffs are managed.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Immune activation may incur fitness costs.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: JA represses PAD4 expression under normal conditions, affecting SA accumulation network behavior.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulator reducing SA accumulation by suppressing EDS5 expression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Growth-immunity intersection factor dampening SA accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DEL1 promotes cell proliferation and reduces SA accumulation. 2. It does so through suppression of EDS5 expression. 3. DEL1 functions at the intersection of plant growth and immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing DEL1 activity could enhance resistance, but may affect growth and cell proliferation.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Illustrates explicit growth-defense tradeoff in SA pathway regulation.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negative regulators/modulators of SA-mediated defense that perceive SA and regulate immune outputs including NPR1 stability or transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major SA receptor pair modulating amplitude of SA signaling
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Cullin3-adaptor-mediated protein degradation and/or SA-inhibited transcriptional repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly modulate SA-responsive gene expression by controlling NPR1 or repressing immune transcription
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: SA-dependent regulatory loop controlling defense amplitude
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NPR3 and NPR4 are SA-binding proteins/receptors functioning as negative regulators of defense. 2. One model proposes they act as cullin3 adaptors promoting NPR1 degradation, while another proposes SA blocks their repression activity independently of NPR1. 3. They also facilitate JAZ degradation in an SA-dependent non-canonical pathway promoting ETI.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: SA-binding assays; genetic analysis; protein interaction and degradation studies
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Fine-tuning NPR3/NPR4 may optimize immunity without constitutive defense, but mechanism remains debated.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Misregulation may impair defense homeostasis or growth balance.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic controversy is explicitly noted in the review.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator of SA-mediated resistance through control of key SA pathway genes
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Transcriptional hub coordinating multiple SA pathway regulators
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NPR1; EDS1; PAD4; ICS1; EDS5; NIMIN2; WRKY38; WRKY62
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Promotes expression of multiple SA defense genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SA signaling and biosynthesis network transcription
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED14/SWP is involved in SA-mediated resistance to Pst DC3000. 2. It regulates expression of multiple core SA genes including NPR1, EDS1, PAD4, ICS1, and EDS5. 3. It therefore acts as a high-level transcriptional coordinator of SA defense.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential systems-level target, though broad transcriptional roles may cause pleiotropy.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely pleiotropic effects due to broad transcriptional function.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Initially linked to cell proliferation before defense function was recognized.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positive regulator essential for systemic acquired resistance and SA-induced immunity
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: SAR and SA/JA-ET defense transcriptional balance
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED16/SFR16 plays an essential role in SAR. 2. It positively regulates SA-mediated immunity against P. syringae. 3. It also contributes to JA-mediated defense against necrotrophs, indicating cross-pathway coordination.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential hub for broad-spectrum resistance, but likely pleiotropic.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Participates in SA-mediated response downstream of NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Downstream transcriptional co-regulator of NPR1 output
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Supports SA-responsive transcription downstream of NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MED15/NRB4 is involved in SA-mediated responses. 2. It functions downstream of NPR1. 3. It likely supports transcriptional activation of SA defense genes.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential downstream tuning point for NPR1 signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No specific direct targets detailed in this review.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: COR/JA-induced repressors of SA accumulation that repress ICS1 and activate BSMT1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct transcriptional switch suppressing SA biosynthesis under coronatine manipulation
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression of ICS1 and activation of BSMT1
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of COR-triggered cascade antagonizing SA pathway
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Coronatine activates MYC2, which induces ANAC019, ANAC055, and ANAC072. 2. These NAC TFs directly repress ICS1 and activate BSMT1. 3. This reduces SA biosynthesis and accumulation, suppressing SA-mediated defense.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing promoter binding or reducing NAC responsiveness could mitigate coronatine-mediated susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: These are host regulators hijacked by pathogen toxin rather than pathogen genes themselves.

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Bacterial type III effector; E3 ubiquitin ligase
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III effector with E3 ligase activity
Regulation_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Host cell; interacts with nuclear/cytosolic NPR1 context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses SA signaling by promoting SA-dependent degradation of NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct pathogen attack on master SA signaling regulator
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Impairment of NPR1-regulated genes including PR1 and callose synthase genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Salicylic acid facilitates interaction with NPR1
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AvrPtoB strongly interacts with NPR1 in the presence of SA. 2. This interaction leads to ubiquitination-mediated degradation of NPR1 dependent on AvrPtoB E3 ligase activity. 3. As a result, NPR1-regulated defense genes are impaired and bacterial pathogenicity is favored.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome-wide effector screen; interaction assays; degradation assays; pathogen infection studies
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major target for genome editing of NPR1 to remove effector interaction or modification sites.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Editing must preserve native NPR1 function.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review proposes SA may promote conformational states enabling AvrPtoB-NPR1 interaction.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses SA-regulated gene expression by degrading MED19a and shifting defense balance toward JA/ET signaling
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Pathogen effector rewiring transcriptional balance away from SA defense
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Decreases SA-regulated gene expression and enhances JA/ET signaling
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Likely acts as adaptor with E3 ligases BOI and MBR1-like
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HaRxL44 is a nuclear-localized downy mildew effector that interacts with MED19a. 2. It triggers proteasome-dependent degradation of MED19a. 3. This suppresses SA-regulated genes and enhances JA/ET signaling, increasing susceptibility.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Interaction assays; degradation analysis; infection phenotyping
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: MED19a editing to prevent HaRxL44 targeting is proposed in the review.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No similarity to known E3 ligases; likely adaptor role suggested.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Suppresses SA-induced antiviral gene silencing by inhibiting AGO1 and AGO4 activities
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Protein interaction-mediated inhibition of slicer activities
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly suppresses RNA silencing-mediated antiviral defense
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CMV 2b suppresses post-transcriptional gene silencing and the miRNA pathway. 2. It physically interacts with AGO1 and AGO4 and inhibits their slicer activities. 3. Expression of CMV2b reduces the inhibitory effect of SA on virus proliferation, supporting a role in blocking SA-induced VIGS.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein interaction assays; transgenic expression; virus proliferation analysis
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering AGO variants resistant to viral suppression.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Could affect endogenous RNA silencing pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Represents viral disruption of an SA-induced downstream defense branch rather than SA biosynthesis itself.
